marketFocus.focus: Tech Sell-Off Stabilizes, Silver Rebounds from 9% Plunge, Central Banks Cut Rates
headlines: Asia-Pac stocks trade mixed after early sell-off stabilizes, tech valuations questioned but losses contained
Silver rebounds from 9% plunge, precious metals claw back losses (spot silver nearly flat, gold recovers)
Central banks cut rates: BoE 25bp (dovish 7-2 split), ECB 25bp (unanimous), GBP lags on dovish signal
marketFocus.macroContext: Markets stabilize after Wednesday's tech rout and silver plunge. Asia-Pac stocks trade mixed as early sell-off gradually contained (Emini S&P -1.0%, Nasdaq -1.7% at session lows but recovering). Precious metals rebound: silver nearly flat after 9% dip, gold claws back from -2.4% retreat. Central banks delivered expected 25bp cuts—BoE dovish split (2 members voted 50bp) weighs on GBP, ECB unanimous decision supports EUR stability. USD holds haven gains (DXY rangebound after gaining on haven appeal), Treasury yields fell on weak US labour data (JOLTs 6.542M vs exp. 7.2M). Geopolitical tensions persist (US-Iran missile deployment, fleet positioning). Focus shifts to Friday's Canadian jobs data (Employment Change exp. 5.2K, Unemployment Rate exp. 6.8%) and US consumer sentiment (Prelim UoM exp. 55.0).

tradingSummary: USD holds haven premium with DXY rangebound after gaining yesterday. GBP weakest G10 currency after dovish BoE split (7-2 vote, 2 members wanted 50bp cut). EUR stable post-ECB 25bp cut (unanimous). Commodities rebound: Silver nearly flat after 9% plunge, Gold recovers from -2.4% retreat. Asia-Pac stocks mixed: early sell-off stabilizes (Emini S&P -1.0%, Nasdaq -1.7% at lows but recovering). T-note futures hold gains after bull steepening on disappointing labour metrics. Risk sentiment: Risk-Off Recovery detected. Key levels: EUR/USD 1.0350 support, GBP/USD 1.2450 support (dovish BoE), USD/JPY 148.50-150.00 range, AUD/USD 0.6250 support (commodity weakness).

USD.summary: USD maintains YTD rebound on safe-haven demand amid tech sell-off and weak US labour data (JOLTs 6.542M vs exp. 7.2M). Treasury yields fell on disappointing labour metrics, but dollar rangebound after gaining on haven appeal. DXY holds gains as all G10 FX trade softer vs USD. Focus shifts to Friday's US UoM Consumer Sentiment (exp. 55.0) and Canadian jobs data.
headlines: US JOLTs Job Openings (Dec) 6.542M vs Exp. 7.2M - weak labour market data
T-note futures hold gains after bull steepening on disappointing labour market metrics
Dollar rangebound after gaining yesterday on haven appeal, DXY holds YTD rebound
Amazon shares dropped ~10% post-earnings on bottom line miss, elevated capex guidance

EUR.summary: EUR little changed after ECB cut rates by 25bp as expected (unanimous decision). Lagarde says inflation path on track to target, medium-term inflation at 2% in 2027-2028. EUR trading marginally lower vs USD but stable vs other G10 currencies. Market focus on Friday's German Industrial Production and French Trade Balance data.
headlines: ECB cut rates by 25bp as expected, unanimous decision, Lagarde says inflation path on track
ECB President Lagarde: Medium-term inflation at 2% in 2027-2028, risks broadly balanced
EUR little changed by ECB meeting, trading marginally lower vs USD but holding steady
Focus on Friday's German Industrial Production and French Trade Balance

GBP.summary: GBP lags after dovish BoE vote split (7-2 for 25bp cut, 2 members voted for 50bp cut). Market interprets split as signal for more aggressive easing ahead. Bailey says MPC exercising judgement on monpol course, mortgage market a key consideration. Political uncertainty adds pressure (Labour MPs urging leadership challenge vs Starmer). GBP weakest G10 currency vs USD.
headlines: BoE cut rates by 25bp (7-2 vote), 2 members voted for 50bp cut - dovish split weighs on GBP
BoE Governor Bailey: MPC exercising judgement on monpol course, mortgage market key consideration
UK Labour MPs urging leadership challenge against Sir Keir Starmer over Lord Mandelson scandal
GBP lags after dovish BoE vote split, weakest G10 currency vs USD on Thursday

JPY.summary: JPY mixed as safe-haven demand competes with risk-off rebound. BoJ's Masu says central bank not behind the curve, monitoring FX impact on inflation. JPY underperforms vs USD but gains vs risk currencies (AUD, NZD). T-note futures hold gains after bull steepening, supporting JPY. Focus on Friday's Japan Household Spending data (exp. -2.6% y/y).
headlines: BoJ's Masu says central bank not behind the curve, monitoring FX impact on inflation
BoJ closely watching FX market moves and their impact on economy and prices, appropriate rate hike needed
JPY mixed, underperforms vs USD but gains vs risk currencies as risk sentiment stabilizes
Focus on Friday's Japan Household Spending (exp. -2.6% y/y)

AUD.summary: AUD mixed as RBA Governor Bullock says Board not happy with inflation prospects, but risk-off rebound supports. RBA held rates at 3.85% (Feb 3), maintaining hawkish stance. Commodity price weakness (silver -9%, gold -2.4%) weighs on AUD. Asia-Pac stocks trade mixed after early sell-off stabilized. Focus on Friday's RBA Gov Bullock speech.
headlines: RBA Governor Bullock says Board not happy with inflation, monitoring closely extent of persistent inflation
Asia-Pac stocks trade mixed after early sell-off gradually stabilised, losses contained
Commodity price weakness (silver -9%, gold -2.4%) weighs on AUD despite RBA hawkish stance
Focus on Friday's RBA Gov Bullock speech

CAD.summary: CAD steady ahead of Friday's Employment Change (exp. 5.2K) and Unemployment Rate (exp. 6.8%). BoC Governor Macklem says restructuring Canadian economy to cope with US tariffs will take years and could be painful. Oil prices stable (WTI ~USD 63/bbl) after settling lower on Wednesday. Focus on Friday's Canadian jobs data and Ivey PMI (exp. 49.7).
headlines: BoC Governor Macklem: Restructuring Canadian economy to cope with US tariffs will take years, could be painful
Focus on Friday's CAD Employment Change (exp. 5.2K) and Unemployment Rate (exp. 6.8%)
CAD steady ahead of Friday's jobs data, oil prices stable (WTI ~USD 63/bbl)
Friday's CAD Ivey PMI (exp. 49.7) also in focus

CHF.summary: CHF steady as safe-haven demand supports amid tech sell-off and risk-off sentiment. SNB rate at 0.00%, no policy changes expected near-term. CHF gains vs risk currencies (AUD, NZD) but lags vs USD. Focus on Friday's CHF Foreign Currency Reserves and Unemployment Rate data.
headlines: CHF gains vs risk currencies amid tech sell-off and risk-off sentiment, lags vs USD
SNB rate at 0.00%, no policy changes expected near-term, CHF safe-haven bid intact
Focus on Friday's CHF Foreign Currency Reserves and Unemployment Rate
CHF steady, safe-haven bid supports but lags vs USD on haven appeal

NZD.summary: NZD weakest among commodity currencies as risk-off sentiment and commodity price weakness weigh. Silver plunge (-9% to -13%) and gold retreat (-2.4%) pressure NZD. NZ Bank Holiday on Friday limits trading activity. RBNZ rate at 2.25%, market expects further easing. NZD underperforms vs all G10 currencies.
headlines: Silver plunge (-9% to -13% from recent highs) pressures NZD heavily, gold retreat (-2.4%)
NZ Bank Holiday on Friday limits trading activity, RBNZ rate at 2.25%
NZD weakest commodity currency, silver plunge and risk-off sentiment weigh heavily
NZD underperforms vs all G10 currencies, market expects further RBNZ easing

volatility.outlook: Volatility remains elevated but stabilizing as Asia-Pac stocks trade mixed after early sell-off. Silver rebounds from 9% dip, precious metals claw back losses. Central banks (BoE, ECB) cut rates by 25bp as expected, but dovish BoE split adds uncertainty. Focus shifts to Friday's Canadian jobs data and US consumer sentiment.

macroContext.narrative: Markets stabilize after Wednesday's tech sell-off and silver plunge. Asia-Pac stocks trade mixed as early losses contained. Precious metals rebound from lows (silver nearly flat after 9% dip, gold recovers from -2.4% retreat). Central banks (BoE, ECB) cut rates by 25bp, but dovish BoE split (2 members voted 50bp) weighs on GBP. USD holds haven gains, Treasury yields fell on weak US labour data (JOLTs 6.542M vs exp. 7.2M). Geopolitical tensions persist (US-Iran missile deployment, fleet positioning). Focus shifts to Friday's Canadian jobs data (Employment Change exp. 5.2K, Unemployment Rate exp. 6.8%) and US consumer sentiment (Prelim UoM exp. 55.0).

tradingSummary: USD holds haven premium with DXY rangebound after gaining yesterday. GBP weakest G10 currency after dovish BoE split (7-2 vote, 2 members wanted 50bp cut). EUR stable post-ECB 25bp cut (unanimous). Commodities rebound: Silver nearly flat after 9% plunge, Gold recovers from -2.4% retreat. Asia-Pac stocks mixed: early sell-off stabilizes (Emini S&P -1.0%, Nasdaq -1.7% at lows but recovering). T-note futures hold gains after bull steepening on disappointing labour metrics. Risk sentiment: Risk-Off Recovery detected. Key levels: EUR/USD 1.0350 support, GBP/USD 1.2450 support (dovish BoE), USD/JPY 148.50-150.00 range, AUD/USD 0.6250 support (commodity weakness). Outlook: Risk sentiment stabilizing but cautious ahead of Friday's Canadian jobs data (Employment Change exp. 5.2K, Unemployment Rate exp. 6.8%) and US consumer sentiment (Prelim UoM exp. 55.0). Tech sell-off stabilizes, silver rebounds from 9% plunge. USD holds haven premium, GBP lags on dovish BoE split.
